title: Smoky Roasted Ethiopian Sugar Dulet
description: Winey, velvety, weeknight Ethiopian dish featuring sugar, smoke, and satvik-friendly ingredients in the form of dulet. Pair with injera or rice for a filling meal.
instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped cilantro, if using, and cook for 1 minute, until wilted.
In a small bowl, mix together the berbere spice mix, sugar, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper, if using.
Add the spice mixture to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped sorrel leaves, if using, and cook until wilted.
Add the browned butter, if using, and stir to combine.
Stir in the honey, vinegar, lemon juice, and oregano, if using.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Transfer the mixture to a serving dish and garnish with cilantro leaves, if using.
Serve hot, garnished with sorrel leaves, if desired.
Serve with injera or rice for a filling meal.
